content.full_text:
State of California

M e m o r a n d u m

To : File                                          Date : January 19, 1989


From : FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
       John McLean

Subject : T-88-462


       Spoke to Senator Roberti in late December.  Advised him that
he could pay campaign workers in 1989 from restricted funds for
vacation time earned in 1988.


JGM:ld:jmmemo2

FPPC

December 8, 1988
DEC 8 3:00 PM '88


Ms. Alice Hughes
Staff Services Analyst
Fair Political Practices Commission
428 J Street, Suite 800
Sacramento, CA 95814

Dear Ms. Hughes:

I have the following questions to ask of the Fair Political
Practices Commission.

I give employees of my campaign committee earned vacation
time as roughly the same basis they would earn in state
employment.

May I pay for vacation time earned in 1988 but not taken
until 1989 out of pre-Proposition 73 funds that have not
been "segregated"?

Would there be a difference if I paid for the accrued vacation
time in 1988 even if the vacation were not taken until 1989?

Sincerely,

DAVID ROBERTI

DR/po

California
Fair Political
Practices Commission

December 12, 1988


Honorable David Roberti
State Senate
State Capitol, Room 205
Sacramento, CA 95814

Re: 88-462

Dear Senator Roberti:

Your letter requesting advice under the Political Reform
Act was received on December 8, 1988 by the Fair Political
Practices Commission. If you have any questions about your
advice request, you may contact Kathryn Donovan, an attorney in
the Legal Division, directly at (916) 322-5901.

We try to answer all advice requests promptly. Therefore,
unless your request poses particularly complex legal questions,
or more information is needed, you should expect a response
within 21 working days if your request seeks formal written
advice. If more information is needed, the person assigned to
prepare a response to your request will contact you shortly to
advise you as to information needed. If your request is for
informal assistance, we will answer it as quickly as we can.
(See Commission Regulation 18329 (2 Cal. Code of Regs. Sec.
18329).)

You also should be aware that your letter and our response
are public records which may be disclosed to the public upon
receipt of a proper request for disclosure.

Very truly yours,

[signature]
Diane M. Griffiths
General Counsel

DMG:plh


428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804-0807 • (916) 322-5660

State of California

Memorandum

To        :    File                                     Date  :  January 19, 1989


From  :  FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
         John McLean

Subject :  T-88-462


         Spoke to Senator Roberti in late December.  Advised him that
he could pay campaign workers in 1989 from restricted funds for
vacation time earned in 1988.


JGM:ld:jmmemo2

December 8, 1988

Ms. Alice Hughes
Staff Services Analyst
Fair Political Practices Commission
428 J Street, Suite 800
Sacramento, CA 95814

Dear Ms. Hughes:

I have the following questions to ask of the Fair Political
Practices Commission.

I give employees of my campaign committee earned vacation
time as roughly the same basis they would earn in state
employment.

May I pay for vacation time earned in 1988 but not taken
until 1989 out of pre-Proposition 73 funds that have not
been "segregated"?

Would there be a difference if I paid for the accrued vacation
time in 1988 even if the vacation were not taken until 1989?

Sincerely,

DAVID ROBERTI

DR/po

sections.question: I give employees of my campaign committee earned vacation time on roughly the same basis they would earn in state employment. May I pay for vacation time earned in 1988 but not taken until 1989 out of pre-Proposition 73 funds that have not been "segregated"? Would there be a difference if I paid for the accrued vacation time in 1988 even if the vacation were not taken until 1989?
sections.conclusion: Advised him that he could pay campaign workers in 1989 from restricted funds for vacation time earned in 1988.
sections.question_synthetic: Senator Roberti asked whether he could pay campaign workers for vacation time earned in 1988 but taken in 1989 using pre-Proposition 73 funds, and whether the timing of payment would affect the analysis.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC advised Senator Roberti that campaign workers could be paid in 1989 from restricted funds for vacation time earned in 1988.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document includes multiple copies of the same materials. Informal advice given via telephone conversation in late December 1988, with formal response letter template sent December 12, 1988, and informal memorandum documenting the advice dated January 19, 1989. No formal conclusion/short answer section in document structure.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: I give employees of my campaign committee earned vacation time on roughly the same basis they would earn in state employment. May I pay for vacation time earned in 1988 but not taken until 1989 out of pre-Proposition 73 funds that have not been "segregated"? Would there be a difference if I paid for the accrued vacation time in 1988 even if the vacation were not taken until 1989?

CONCLUSION: Advised him that he could pay campaign workers in 1989 from restricted funds for vacation time earned in 1988.

embedding.summary: Senator David Roberti requested FPPC advice on whether he could pay campaign workers for accrued vacation time earned in 1988 but taken in 1989 using pre-Proposition 73 funds. The FPPC's informal advice confirmed that he could pay campaign workers in 1989 from restricted funds for vacation time earned in 1988.
